commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Lack of hover states or subtle transitions on the forecast strip elements.
issues.fix: Add a subtle hover effect (e.g., slight lift or background color change) to each forecast day item to indicate interactivity.

issues.description: The wind speed and humidity stats could benefit from small icons to improve scannability.
issues.fix: Add small icon components (e.g., Droplet for humidity, Wind for wind speed) next to the labels.

issues.description: The forecast list lacks a structural semantic role, making it difficult for screen readers to interpret as a grouped list.
issues.fix: Wrap the forecast map in a <ul> element and each item in an <li> with appropriate role attributes or use a semantic ARIA list structure.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, uses the design system appropriately, and renders data cleanly. It functions well as a display component, though it lacks the micro-interactions and semantic grouping needed for a truly high-end production UI.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid structure and proper use of design tokens, but lacks visual polish with missing hover/transition states and the forecast strip doesn't have adequate responsive behavior for smaller screens. The temperature display could benefit from a weather emoji/icon alongside the numeric value as mentioned in requirements.
